Products.Headline: Everything about the new Reign Advanced E+ is designed to make it the best descending E-MTB ever. From the bottomless feeling Maestro suspension with 170mm of rear travel (and a 180mm fork) to the punchy new SyncDrive Pro 3 motor and its lightweight, stiff and ultra-adjustable composite frame. We added a titanium skid plate, robust frame protection, and an innovative bash-guard with an elastomer damper—so you can send it with confidence on the most rugged, technical terrain.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: DOMINANT ON DESCENTS
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Featuring a modified Maestro suspension system with a new integrated lower link and 170mm of rear travel paired with a 180mm fork, this is best descending E-bike ever from Giant. Maestro’s floating pivot design means it’s always active and efficient under pedaling. Its progressive leverage curve makes the rear shock is easy to compress at the top of the stroke and super-supportive near the end range, producing a plush, bottomless feel that uses every bit of its travel. The result is unmatched confidence and control for big hits, steep drops and technical rock gardens.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: LIGHTWEIGHT YET POWERFUL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Upgrading from a 36-volt to 48-volt system results in better performance with reduced heat and energy loss. With 90Nm of torque and 800 watts of peak power, the new SyncDrive Pro 3 motor lets you sustain a high torque level, even at higher pedaling cadences—so you feel powerful, consistent support in any riding situation The added power combined with a lighter composite frame and overall weight of just 22.3kg (size medium) means the Reign E+ Advanced packs a punch when you need it most. And the 48-volt system also produces less stress on the EnergyPak 560 battery, which has a nearly endless lifespan of up to 2300 charge cycles.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: PREMIUM PROTECTION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Designed to shred hard and take a beating, the Reign Advanced E+ features a tough, dual-density battery cover that doubles as a full-length protector on the underside of the down tube—perfect for chairlifts, trailers or pickup truck shuttles. A new integrated skid plate protects the motor with corrosion resistant titanium that’s twice as strong as aluminum. And with less friction, the skid plate also helps keep you from getting hung up on rocks, roots, or other obstacles. Finally, a unique MRP active bash-guard, with an elastomer damper and suspended design, gives you unmatched chain and chainring protection.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: E+ System
Technologies.Description:
E+ System combines electric power with human power to produce a smart, natural and powerful E-bike riding experience. It’s an integrated group of technologies that includes our RideControl control units, EnergyPak battery systems and SyncDrive motors.
For more information, check out the link below.

Technologies.Name: Flip Chip - Maestro 3
Technologies.Description: This all-new technology allows riders to quickly adjust the geometry and choose a rear wheel size (29-inch or 27.5-inch) for their riding style and terrain. It’s available on select Maestro-equipped mountain bikes. The adjustment affects head tube and seat tube angles, bottom bracket height, and rear-wheel size using eccentric flip chip hardware located on the upper rocker arm of the rear suspension.

Technologies.Name: Maestro Suspension Technology
Technologies.Description: Four strategically positioned pivot points and two linkages all work together to create a single floating pivot for the most active, efficient and independent suspension system on the trail.

Technologies.Name: RideControl Ergo 4
Technologies.Description: The new RideControl Ergo 4 builds on the previous generation Ergo 3 with a sleek, grip-integrated 3-button remote control unit. It blends seamlessly with your handlebar and grips and has a larger, more concave shape and improved tactile feedback.

Technologies.Name: Energypak 560
Technologies.Description: Integrated 560Wh battery pack powers a 48-volt system that produces more power with less heat and stress on the battery cells. Exceptionally durable, it delivers up to 2300 full charge cycles—more than any other E-bike battery—and recharges rapidly, reaching 80% battery capacity in as little as two hours.

Technologies.Name: SyncDrive Pro 3
Technologies.Description: Powerful new 48-volt system delivers up to 800 watts of peak power while producing less heat and motor stress—improving efficiency to give you more peak power for longer. You can tune and adjust torque and support levels to match your ride. Choose full power for steep climbs, or dial it back to save energy on long rides.

Technologies.Name: EnergyPak Plus 280
Technologies.Description: This range extender can boost total capacity by 280Wh. This gives you the choice—lighter weight for everyday rides or longer range for epic ride days.

Products.BikeSeriesDescription: Whether you’re riding trails, exploring dirt roads, or putting some zip in your daily commute, riding an E-bike doesn’t have to be complicated. The all-new Talon E+ range lets you do it all, combining the exhilarating sensation of pedal-assist power with light and agile off-road handling. And its integrated system makes it all so seamless, so simple to use, you can forget about the technology and just enjoy the ride.
Products.Headline: Less weight. More Fun

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SyncDrive Sport 2
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: This frame is paired with a SyncDrive Sport 2 motor and removable EnergyPak 430 battery that’s neatly integrated into a slim down tube to help minimize weight. The updated design allows space for two water bottles or a battery range extender for longer rides. A 100mm suspension fork helps smooth out bumpy terrain, while confident off-road geometry gives it a stable ride quality.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: RideControl Dash 2
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A new RideControl Dash 2 handlebar-mounted controller makes it easy to power on the bike and adjust support levels while you ride. You can also use the RideControl App to custom-tune the motor output and optimize range capabilities.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Designed Lighter
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The frameset is crafted with lightweight aluminum shaped with sleek lines and modern styling. It weighs just 21.7kg (size medium)—significantly lighter than most other bikes in its category.

Technologies.Name: E+ System
Technologies.Description:
E+ System combines electric power with human power to produce a smart, natural and powerful E-bike riding experience. It’s an integrated group of technologies that includes our RideControl control units, EnergyPak battery systems and SyncDrive motors.
For more information, check out the link below.

Technologies.Name: Energypak
Technologies.Description: Giant’s latest EnergyPak battery systems feature streamlined designs that are integrated with the E-bike for a sleek and modern profile. Giant E-bikes offer several different EnergyPak options including the EnergyPak Smart and EnergyPak. Each one is designed to optimize the E-bike experience for a specific style of riding.

Technologies.Name: SyncDrive Sport 2
Technologies.Description: The compact SyncDrive Sport 2 motor delivers 75Nm of torque tuned for trail riding. It features Smart Assist mode, which uses six sensors to measure your pedaling input, cadence and speed, along with the slope you’re riding on. Based on these calculations, it produces power that feels naturally supportive and in sync with the way you ride. The motor’s compact shape also allows for a narrow Q-factor for optimized pedaling efficiency, comfort and biomechanics.

Technologies.Name: ALUXX SL Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This High-Performance level, state-of-the-art aluminum material is made predominantly from 6011 alloy and delivers best-in-class strength-to-weight ratios. These framesets also feature unique welding technologies for a high-performance ride quality.

Technologies.Name: Find My Bike
Technologies.Description: Enhanced security and anti-theft feature using Apple Find My function lets you set up separation alerts, locate your E-bike remotely, and receive notifications if its location changes. Apple Find My privacy protections ensure that your location information is encrypted on Apple servers and retained for only 24 hours.

Technologies.Name: GIANT E-LOCK SYSTEM
Technologies.Description: Allows you to lock and unlock your E-bike using the RideControl App or by entering a PIN code on the controller. Once locked, pedaling assistance is blocked and the display shows the E-bike in lock status. If someone attempts to move the bike, the alarm is triggered.

Products.MetaDescription: From hors categorie climbs to rollers and flats, this WorldTour level race bike is the pro’s choice for all-rounder performance. The all-new flagship TCR uses system integration and innovative construction methods to deliver a clear advantage on the road.

Products.Headline: From hors categorie climbs to rollers and flats, this WorldTour level race bike is the pro’s choice for all-rounder performance. The all-new flagship TCR uses system integration and innovative construction methods to deliver a clear advantage on the road.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM OPTIMIZATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The frame, fork and integrated seatpost are engineered, developed and tested to perform as a system. The fork features a D-shaped steerer tube with corresponding spacers that, in addition to improving aerodynamics, also allows the cables to be internally routed through the handlebars, stem and frame/fork for a cleaner profile. And the signature ISP saves 40g while also adding road smoothing compliance and further improving its aero performance.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: WINNING EFFICIENCY
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Featuring a greater stiffness-to-weight ratio than the previous generation, along with new aero tube shaping and a lighter overall weight, this uncompromising race bike delivers explosive acceleration and unyielding pedaling stiffness. The result is race-winning performance in the moments that count.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: INTEGRATED AERODYNAMICS
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: From the truncated ellipse shape of frameset tubing to a new cockpit design and component integration, it all adds up to a more streamlined profile that helps give the flagship TCR Advanced SL 0 model a 4.19-watt advantage over the previous generation.

Technologies.Name: Advanced SL Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Professional Grade raw carbon fiber is woven in our composite factory to produce our lightest, stiffest and highest quality frames. This is the technology used by Giant’s pro racers in almost every cycling discipline.

Technologies.Name: Integrated Seatpost
Technologies.Description: Designed as a key performance factor in the overall frameset, the integrated seatpost saves up to 40 grams compared to standard composite posts, while improving aerodynamics and adding road-smoothing compliance.

To achieve this, we designed, developed and purpose-built a new range of bikes that stands apart from previous Giant youth road bikes and sets a new standard in the category.

Products.Headline: The new Seek was created for one reason: to transform the road riding experience for kids and give them the same feeling of speed, efficiency and control that adult road riders enjoy. To achieve this, we designed, developed and purpose-built a new range of bikes that stands apart from previous Giant youth road bikes and sets a new standard in the category.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: LIGHT AND AGILE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The Seek features a lightweight ALUXX aluminum frame designed with smaller diameter frame tubing specifically for young riders. The frame and composite fork, which adds road-smoothing compliance and precise steering control, work together to produce a dynamic ride quality.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: YOUTH- OPTIMIZED COMPONENTS
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The cockpit is designed to give smaller riders greater efficiency and control on the road. The Giant Contact SL Lite handlebar is specially shaped with a shorter reach (62mm) and drops (100mm) compared to typical adult bars. The brake levers are also designed for smaller hands, with a reach that’s 35% shorter from the drops and 30% shorter from the hoods. The outer hood diameter is 20% smaller, and the lever’s flared shape improves ergonomics for small hands.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: EFFORTLESS FIT
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Designed for kids 130-150cm tall, Seek makes it easy to dial in a perfect fit and make adjustments as the young rider grows taller and gains more experience with road riding. From first timers to junior road racers, it’s an adaptable road bike that evolves with the rider. Beyond the purpose-built frameset designed with smaller diameter tubing, the Seek features a seatpost that’s marked with a body height index, making saddle height adjustment intuitive and simple.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: ALUXX Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This Performance level frame material features 6061 alloy with single-butted tubes to produce strong, lightweight framesets for a variety of different bikes and riding styles.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Seatpost
Technologies.Description: The proprietary “D” shaped composite seatpost damps vibration, resulting in a smooth, compliant ride.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: Dynamic Balanced Lacing
Technologies.Description: Since its introduction in 2015, Dynamic Balanced Lacing (DBL) has been the cornerstone of Giant WheelSystems. DBL ensures that spokes are set for optimal tension when under dynamic load, resulting in enhanced durability and pedaling efficiency. The enlarged DBL hub flange also increases the spoke bracing angles, which boosts overall torsional/transmission and lateral stiffness for better power transfer and more stable sprinting.

Products.Name: Seek 2
Products.MetaDescription: The new Seek was created for one reason: to transform the road riding experience for kids and give them the same feeling of speed, efficiency and control that adult road riders enjoy. To achieve this, we designed, developed and purpose-built a new range of bikes that stands apart from previous Giant youth road bikes and sets a new standard in the category.

Products.Headline: Developed to help Giant pro Alan Hatherly win the 2025 Elite Men’s XCO World Championship, this superlight XC composite flyer is engineered with a semi-integrated 120mm FlexPoint Pro rear suspension system and a flip chip to adjust geometry for any racecourse or XC terrain.
Products.Tagline: A pro-level XC race machine built to shred singletrack and aim for the podium.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TOTAL CONTROL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The new FlexPoint Pro suspension delivers 120mm of smooth, active rear travel paired with a 120mm fork—helping you fly down rough descents and power up technical climbs. A flip chip lets you fine tune handling for all types of XC terrain. Superlight composite or alloy Giant WheelSystems (depending on model) with a next-generation 120-tooth helical ratchet drive system for immediate power and robust 5mm rim sidewalls for greater protection against impacts and punctures.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: LOW-LATENCY SPEED CONCEPT
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A fully reengineered composite frameset is 89 grams lighter than it predecessor, with 7.95 percent greater stiffness-to-weight ratio (efficiency) to help you power up climbs and make every watt count. The WheelSystems and integrated handlebar/stem units are designed for maximum efficiency and instantaneous response to rider input. The rear shock is repositioned and semi-integrated with the top tube, shaving weight and creating space for a second water bottle cage.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A new one-piece integrated handlebar/stem unit adds compliance to reduce shocks and vibrations, improving control and hand comfort. It’s available in two models—the Contact SLR XC Integrated composite and Contact SL XC Integrated alloy. Both feature the same seamless cable routing and versatile sizing with a 780mm standard width and stem lengths ranging from 50-90mm.

Technologies.Name: Advanced SL Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Professional Grade raw carbon fiber is woven in our composite factory to produce our lightest, stiffest and highest quality frames. This is the technology used by Giant’s pro racers in almost every cycling discipline.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Wide Guard Technology
Technologies.Description: When your wheel hits a sharp or square edge, especially at speed, pinch flats and rim damage can occur. Wide Guard technology minimizes this risk by broadening the contact point between the rim and tire with a solid 5mm rim sidewall. That means you can skip the heavy tire inserts to have less unsprung weight on your bike and allow your suspension to perform better. The wide sidewalls also better protect your wheel from big impacts, reducing the possibility of rim damage.

Technologies.Name: MegaDrive
Technologies.Description: A massive rectangular downtube and oversized toptube work in unison to provide superior front end lateral and torsional steering precision. The precisely engineered, rectangular shaped downtube yields unprecedented steering and pedaling stiffness.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Forged Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: A state-of-the-art high-pressure molding process is used to produce complex-shaped carbon fiber components that are lighter, stiffer and stronger than similar components made from aluminum. This technology is used in critical performance parts such as Maestro Suspension rocker links.

Technologies.Name: FlexPoint Pro Suspension Technology
Technologies.Description: Developed as a superlight rear suspension setup for XC bikes that demand efficiency, control and responsive handling, FlexPoint Pro is a linkage-driven, single-pivot system that delivers 100mm of rear-wheel travel. It’s engineered with a full-composite swingarm that significantly reduces overall frame weight.

The lightweight composite frame features a new semi-integrated FlexPoint Pro rear suspension setup with 120mm of travel plus a flip chip to adjust geometry. Combined with a 130mm fork and an integrated handlebar with clean cable routing, it’s fast, fun and ready to rip.
Products.Tagline: A lightweight, efficient mountain bike for XC and fast trail riding.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: RACE-BRED TRAIL SPEED
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A full-composite frameset is both ultra-light and highly efficient to help you power up climbs and make every watt count. The WheelSystems and integrated handlebar/stem units are designed for maximum efficiency and instantaneous response to rider input. The rear shock is repositioned and semi-integrated with the top tube, shaving weight and creating space for a second water bottle cage.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TOTAL CONTROL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The new FlexPoint Pro suspension delivers 120mm of smooth, active rear travel paired with a 130mm fork—helping you fly down rough descents and power up technical climbs. A flip chip lets you fine tune handling for all types of terrain. Superlight composite or alloy Giant WheelSystems (depending on model) with a next-generation 120-tooth helical ratchet drive system for immediate power and robust 5mm rim sidewalls for greater protection against impacts and punctures.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A new one-piece integrated handlebar/stem unit adds compliance to reduce shocks and vibrations, improving control and hand comfort. It’s available in two models—the Contact SLR XC Integrated composite and Contact SL XC Integrated alloy. Both feature the same seamless cable routing and versatile sizing with a 780mm standard width and stem lengths ranging from 50-90mm.

Products.Headline: Break out of the box and expand your road riding adventures. From smooth pavement to bumpy backroads, this ultra-capable road bike has built-in compliance and high-volume tires, so you can ride any road with confidence and control.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Balanced road geometry
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Frame is specifically engineered to produce a balanced, all-rounder feel that blends confident rider positioning with agile cornering and acceleration.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Smooth ride quality
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Innovative D-Fuse seatpost reduces road shocks and vibrations, and a Giant WheelSystem lets you run tubeless tires up to 38mm for a smoother ride quality and reduced risk of flats.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Total control
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Seamless disc brake integration, including flat mounts and 12mm front and rear thru-axles, produces more consistent braking power for better control, even in wet weather and on rough roads.

Technologies.Name: ALUXX Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This Performance level frame material features 6061 alloy with single-butted tubes to produce strong, lightweight framesets for a variety of different bikes and riding styles.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Functional simplicity
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Free of cumbersome gimmickry, the frameset is one of the lightest in its category. With a perfect balance of high-performance reactivity and all-day comfort, it offers a “less is more” experience for modern endurance road riding.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Engineered compliance
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Advanced-grade composite frame and fork are specifically engineered for endurance road riding, delivering a smooth and efficient ride quality that minimizes fatigue on long rides. The new D-Fuse components work together to further absorb road shocks and vibrations.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Total control
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: New Contact D-Fuse handlebar features ergonomic shaping and an 8-degree flare for comfort and control on all-day rides. Wide, alloy rims are optimized for high-volume tubeless tires (up to 38mm), providing predictable handling and stability from asphalt to cobbles.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive Aero
Technologies.Description: Found inside the head tube of the Defy, Propel and TCR ranges, the D-shaped fork steerer tube and corresponding spacers provide an extremely aerodynamic, easier to service system of internally routed brake and derailleur cables.

Products.Headline: Ride roads, gravel or dirt with this versatile aluminum all-rounder. A composite fork gives it a smooth ride, and a flip chip lets you adjust wheelbase and tire clearance.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Confident handling
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Frame is designed with endurance positioning and shorter chainstays for precise handling, agility and control. A longer reach, shorter stem and lower bottom bracket drop improves stability on rugged terrain.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Tuned compliance
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Dropped seatstays with thin tubing absorb road shocks and vibrations. The D-Fuse handlebar and seatpost work as a system to add compliance without sacrificing stiffness while cornering, sprinting or climbing.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Adaptable to the terrain
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A flip chip on the rear dropout adjusts the wheelbase: short for quicker handling and acceleration, or long for improved stability at speed. The long setting allows for larger diameter tires up to 53mm. You can choose the included D-Fuse seatpost for maximum compliance, or switch to a 30.9mm round or dropper post.

Technologies.Name: ALUXX Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This Performance level frame material features 6061 alloy with single-butted tubes to produce strong, lightweight framesets for a variety of different bikes and riding styles.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: Flip Chip - Dropout
Technologies.Description: Allows riders to quickly adjust the geometry and rear-wheel tire clearance with eccentric, offset two-position hardware in the rear wheel dropouts.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Products.MetaDescription: Rough roads, gravel or dirt. However you choose to roll, this bike does it all. The lightweight, smooth-riding composite frameset and adjustable wheelbase make it more capable than ever.

Products.BikeSeriesDescription: Now lighter, smoother and more adaptable to variable conditions and terrain, this reengineered composite gravel bike combines ultra-efficient performance with next-level handling and control.
Products.Headline: GRAVEL CRUSHER

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: FAST AND VERSATILE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Lightweight Advanced-grade composite frame and Advanced SL composite fork combine for race-winning efficiency and smooth control. A two-position flip chip on the rear dropout lets you adjust the wheelbase: short for quicker handling, or long for added tire clearance (up to 53mm) and improved stability. You can use the included D-Fuse seatpost for more flex and compliance, or switch to a traditional 30.9mm round seatpost or dropper post.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TUNED COMPLIANCE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Dropped seatstays with small-diameter tubing absorb road shocks and vibrations on all types of gravel. The Contact SLR XR D-Fuse carbon handlebar and D-Fuse SLR seatpost help smooth out rough tracks, improving control and minimizing fatigue over long distances.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SMART INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Internally routed cables offer clean, uncluttered cockpit aesthetics and improved aero performance. An easily accessed storage compartment inside the top tube lets you securely carry tools, snacks and other essentials. And additional integrated mounts on the top tube, fork and rear dropout make it easy to modify with additional packs  or fenders. All-new, molded, chainstay and down tube protection help defend the frame against impacts and quell noise.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: MegaDrive
Technologies.Description: A massive rectangular downtube and oversized toptube work in unison to provide superior front end lateral and torsional steering precision. The precisely engineered, rectangular shaped downtube yields unprecedented steering and pedaling stiffness.

Technologies.Name: Flip Chip - Dropout
Technologies.Description: Allows riders to quickly adjust the geometry and rear-wheel tire clearance with eccentric, offset two-position hardware in the rear wheel dropouts.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Products.Headline: Nothing beats that feeling of being one with the trail. Stance helps you tap into it, delivering smooth control with a new aluminum frameset and 130mm of FlexPoint rear suspension featuring a new semi-integrated design. Paired with a 140mm fork and trail-ready features, including a dropper post, it’s everything you need to reach the next level.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TRAIL-FRIENDLY CONTROL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Refined trail geometry with a slacker head tube and higher stack creates a stable, confident ride position. The new Contact Switch AT dropper post and long-post compatibility (up to 200mm) gives you room to move on descents. A wide-range 1X drivetrain, powerful 4-piston brakes, and tubeless tires deliver simple shifting, precise speed control and planted traction—so you don’t just ride the trail, you master it.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TRAIL-TUNED SUSPENSION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Re-engineered FlexPoint rear suspension with a Fox Rhythm TR shock delivers active, consistent performance through 130mm of travel. The single-pivot system boasts the lowest rear-triangle deformation in its class, while new kinematics with tailored shock spring curve deliver a more linear wheel path. The result is a smooth, predictable feel with unbeatable traction and mid-stroke support. Up front, the dual-air Giant STL 34 suspension fork smooths out rocks and roots. And our Unified Suspension Setup makes it easy to dial-in the settings.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: INTEGRATED CHASSIS
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A new hydroformed ALUXX aluminum chassis is engineered for strength, stiffness, and low weight. Seamless shock integration enhances frame stiffness and delivers amore connected ride feel. A smooth cockpit-to-frame transition with integrated spacers keeps the front end clean, while flush cable guides help ensure quiet performance. Convertible internal/external routing adds adaptability. The result is a sleek, precise, trail-ready frame that rides as sharp as it looks

Technologies.Name: ALUXX Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This Performance level frame material features 6061 alloy with single-butted tubes to produce strong, lightweight framesets for a variety of different bikes and riding styles.

Technologies.Name: FlexPoint Suspension Technology
Technologies.Description: A proven “single-pivot” design that uses the flexing action of the chainstay/seatstay junction to produce a lightweight and durable suspension system with 4.7 inches/120mm of rear travel.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: RUGGED DURABILITY
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Super strong and lightweight ALUXX aluminum frameset is backed by a warranty for hard-charging durability. A 26-inch Giant TRA 2 wheelset features Wide Guard rim technology, allowing lower tire pressures for better control along with increased puncture protection.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: AGILITY AND CONTROL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The frame’s lower stand-over height offers balance and stability with a lower center of gravity. The 26-inch wheels and steeper head tube, combined with a shorter wheelbase and chainstays, make it quick and easily maneuverable for dirt jump performance. Gyro compatibility enables 360-degree handlebar rotation for bar spins.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: READY FOR LANDING
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The 135x10mm horizontal sliding rear dropout and Marzocchi Bomber DJ fork with 100mm of travel deliver smooth control on rugged terrain, jumps or drops—making it a versatile choice for dirt jumps or technical trails. A Giant Contact TR35 handlebar and shorter stem deliver pinpoint control that stands up to hard-charging dirt sessions.

Products.Headline: Winning race bikes have just the right balance of light weight, superior stiffness and smooth compliance on the road. TCR Advanced delivers on all three.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Class-leading efficiency
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A high stiffness-to-weight ratio combined with strategically placed aero tube shaping results in faster climbing, explosive acceleration and unyielding pedaling stiffness for race-winning performance.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Advanced aerodynamics
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: This is where the TCR’s most quantifiable gains can be found compared to previous generation models. Every tube shape was analyzed, engineered and tested to create an overall structure with significantly lower drag at a wider range of yaw angles.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: Total control
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A composite fork delivers torsional stiffness for sharp handling and steering precision, while a Giant WheelSystem with tubeless technology allows you to run lower tire pressures for increased grip and a reduced risk of punctures.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Products.Headline: Superlight, ultra-efficient and rides like a dream. This well-rounded race bike blends industry-leading composite frame engineering with fully integrated components to help you climb, corner and descend with astonishing speed and sublime road handling.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM OPTIMIZATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The frameset, wheels, cockpit and other parts are engineered, developed and tested together to perform as a system. The all-new OverDrive Aero steerer tube technology is combined with a Contact SL AeroLight stem and Contact SL handlebar to incorporate internal cable routing that integrates the top cap, cables and spacers in a more aerodynamic, user-friendly setup.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: WINNING EFFICIENCY
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Featuring a greater stiffness-to-weight ratio than the previous generation, along with new aero tube shaping and a lighter overall weight, this uncompromising race bike delivers explosive acceleration and unyielding pedaling stiffness. The result is race-winning performance in the moments that count.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: INTEGRATED AERODYNAMICS
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: All tube shapes are engineered and developed using Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) modeling and wind-tunnel testing. From the truncated ellipse shape of frameset tubing to a new cockpit design and component integration, it all adds up to a more streamlined profile that improves overall aero performance compared to the previous generation.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive Aero
Technologies.Description: Found inside the head tube of the Defy, Propel and TCR ranges, the D-shaped fork steerer tube and corresponding spacers provide an extremely aerodynamic, easier to service system of internally routed brake and derailleur cables.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive Aero
Technologies.Description: Found inside the head tube of the Defy, Propel and TCR ranges, the D-shaped fork steerer tube and corresponding spacers provide an extremely aerodynamic, easier to service system of internally routed brake and derailleur cables.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Compact Road Design
Technologies.Description: Introduced by Giant with the professional Team ONCE in the 1990s, Compact Road revolutionized the look and feel of high-performance road bikes forever. A downward sloping toptube (from the headtube to the seattube) creates smaller front and rear triangles for a lighter, stiffer bike. It also makes it easier for riders of all sizes to have a perfect fit.

Products.Headline: With its smooth-riding composite frame, lightweight battery, and new SyncDrive Move Plus integrated rear hub motor, the Defy Advanced E+ is the ideal choice for performance-minded road riders who want to tackle steep hills, maintain higher speeds, and experience road riding with no limitations.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SEAMLESS POWER
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The SyncDrive Move Plus motor is integrated into the rear hub and delivers 30Nm of power (equivalent to 75Nm with a mid-drive motor) directly to the rear wheel. Weighing just 1.7kg, it’s specially tuned for the pedaling pattern of performance road riding, with minimal energy loss and more torque at higher speeds. The result is a natural feel when accelerating or maintaining steady power at speed, and no motor drag while freewheeling or coasting.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The down tube integrated EnergyPak 400 battery system weighs just 2.3kg and is purpose-built for the specific needs of E-bikes. A top tube mounted RideControl Go button makes it quick and easy to turn the E-bike on or off and change power modes. Smart Assist technology uses three sensors to precisely measure the power being applied to the pedals and produce just the right amount of support for a smooth, natural ride experience. All models feature SRAM wireless electronic shifters and an integrated taillight.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: PURE ROAD PERFORMANCE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A lightweight composite frameset and high-performance components including a D-Fuse seatpost and handlebars for compliance, integrated WheelSystems with tubeless tires, and a wireless electronic drivetrain all add up to a smooth, efficient ride quality that mirrors the award-winning performance of the Defy endurance road bike range.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: E+ System
Technologies.Description:
E+ System combines electric power with human power to produce a smart, natural and powerful E-bike riding experience. It’s an integrated group of technologies that includes our RideControl control units, EnergyPak battery systems and SyncDrive motors.
For more information, check out the link below.

Technologies.Name: Energypak
Technologies.Description: Giant’s latest EnergyPak battery systems feature streamlined designs that are integrated with the E-bike for a sleek and modern profile. Giant E-bikes offer several different EnergyPak options including the EnergyPak Smart and EnergyPak. Each one is designed to optimize the E-bike experience for a specific style of riding.

Technologies.Name: Smart Assist
Technologies.Description: All SyncDrive motors have Smart Assist technology, which adjusts the support to match the rider’s needs. By using information from multiple sensors, both rider input and terrain feedback are used to determine the optimal amount of pedaling assistance. SyncDrive is the only e-bike system that utilizes both rider input and terrain feedback. It gives you a smooth, natural riding experience with ample power when you need it, while also conserving power when you don’t need as much.

Technologies.Name: SyncDrive Move Plus
Technologies.Description: The new SyncDrive Move Plus motor delivers 30Nm of torque, which is comparable to 75Nm of torque in a mid-drive motor—all in a lightweight, quiet system that’s fully integrated into the rear hub. It features five support levels plus Smart Assist technology, which can automatically adjust pedaling support without having to change settings while riding. The motor uses sensors to constantly adjust output based on advanced algorithms.

Technologies.Name: RideControl App
Technologies.Description: The updated RideControl App expands the existing customization options for your SyncDrive motor. Now you choose exactly how you want the system to perform. You can use the app to adjust from the battery-saving Eco mode all the way up to Power mode, and collect all relevant ride metrics during your activity.

Products.Headline: With its smooth-riding composite frame, lightweight battery, and new SyncDrive Move Plus integrated rear hub motor, the Defy Advanced E+ is the ideal choice for performance-minded road riders who want to tackle steep hills, maintain higher speeds, and experience road riding with no limitations.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SEAMLESS POWER
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The SyncDrive Move Plus motor is integrated into the rear hub and delivers 30Nm of power (equivalent to 75Nm with a mid-drive motor) directly to the rear wheel. Weighing just 1.7kg, it’s specially tuned for the pedaling pattern of performance road riding, with minimal energy loss and more torque at higher speeds. The result is a natural feel when accelerating or maintaining steady power at speed, and no motor drag while freewheeling or coasting.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SYSTEM INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: The down tube integrated EnergyPak 400 battery system weighs just 2.3kg and is purpose-built for the specific needs of E-bikes. A top tube mounted RideControl Go button makes it quick and easy to turn the E-bike on or off and change power modes. Smart Assist technology uses three sensors to precisely measure the power being applied to the pedals and produce just the right amount of support for a smooth, natural ride experience. All models feature SRAM wireless electronic shifters and an integrated taillight.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: PURE ROAD PERFORMANCE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: A lightweight composite frameset and high-performance components including a D-Fuse seatpost and handlebars for compliance, integrated WheelSystems with tubeless tires, and a wireless electronic drivetrain all add up to a smooth, efficient ride quality that mirrors the award-winning performance of the Defy endurance road bike range.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: Energypak
Technologies.Description: Giant’s latest EnergyPak battery systems feature streamlined designs that are integrated with the E-bike for a sleek and modern profile. Giant E-bikes offer several different EnergyPak options including the EnergyPak Smart and EnergyPak. Each one is designed to optimize the E-bike experience for a specific style of riding.

Technologies.Name: E+ System
Technologies.Description:
E+ System combines electric power with human power to produce a smart, natural and powerful E-bike riding experience. It’s an integrated group of technologies that includes our RideControl control units, EnergyPak battery systems and SyncDrive motors.
For more information, check out the link below.

Technologies.Name: RideControl App
Technologies.Description: The updated RideControl App expands the existing customization options for your SyncDrive motor. Now you choose exactly how you want the system to perform. You can use the app to adjust from the battery-saving Eco mode all the way up to Power mode, and collect all relevant ride metrics during your activity.

Technologies.Name: Smart Assist
Technologies.Description: All SyncDrive motors have Smart Assist technology, which adjusts the support to match the rider’s needs. By using information from multiple sensors, both rider input and terrain feedback are used to determine the optimal amount of pedaling assistance. SyncDrive is the only e-bike system that utilizes both rider input and terrain feedback. It gives you a smooth, natural riding experience with ample power when you need it, while also conserving power when you don’t need as much.

Technologies.Name: SyncDrive Move Plus
Technologies.Description: The new SyncDrive Move Plus motor delivers 30Nm of torque, which is comparable to 75Nm of torque in a mid-drive motor—all in a lightweight, quiet system that’s fully integrated into the rear hub. It features five support levels plus Smart Assist technology, which can automatically adjust pedaling support without having to change settings while riding. The motor uses sensors to constantly adjust output based on advanced algorithms.

Products.Headline: From its sleek, aero-inspired alloy frame and composite fork to its road-smoothing components and ergonomically designed cockpit, the new Contend SL makes performance road riding accessible to all. Speed through the city or join the group ride with more confidence, control and speed.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: AERO INSPIRED
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: An aero-shaped down tube and composite fork form a sleek profile that cuts through the wind. The Giant ALP WheelSystem has an all-rounder rim depth of 30mm and robust aluminum construction that’s lightweight and strong enough to handle everyday use on variable road surfaces. The cockpit setup features internally routed cables to further reduce wind drag and create a clean, modern look.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: COMMAND CONTROL
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: An ergonomically designed handlebar optimizes hand comfort in every position, from the tops to the hoods to the drops. The drops have 7.2-degree flare for greater confidence and control descending and cornering. Hydraulic disc brakes deliver consistent, predictable braking power and help minimize hand fatigue on long descents. A proven Shimano drivetrain with a 50/34 chainring combination offers a wide range of gears for optimal pedaling cadence on variable terrain.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SMOOTH COMFORT
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: An Advanced-grade composite fork soaks up the bumps and contours of the road, minimizing hand and arm fatigue, while an ergo-shaped handlebar with an expanded surface area helps distributes pressure across the palm and thumb web. A D-Fuse seatpost absorbs shocks and vibrations, and a Giant Grit Core saddle uses Particle Flow Technology to conform to your body. Higher volume 28mm tubeless tires allow you to run lower tire pressure for better grip and a smoother ride quality.

Technologies.Name: ALUXX SL Aluminum Technology
Technologies.Description: This High-Performance level, state-of-the-art aluminum material is made predominantly from 6011 alloy and delivers best-in-class strength-to-weight ratios. These framesets also feature unique welding technologies for a high-performance ride quality.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Products.Headline: A light, smooth and adaptable to variable conditions and terrain, the special edition Revolt Advanced SE combines ultra-efficient performance, next-level control, and striking colors and graphics that make it stand out in the pack.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: FAST AND VERSATILE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: An Advanced-grade composite frame and fork combine for lightweight efficiency and smooth control. A two-position flip chip on the rear dropout lets you adjust the wheelbase: short for quicker handling, or long for added tire clearance (up to 53mm) and improved stability. You can use the included D-Fuse seatpost for more flex and compliance, or switch to a traditional 30.9mm round seatpost or dropper post.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: TUNED COMPLIANCE
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Dropped seatstays with small-diameter tubing absorb road shocks and vibrations on all types of gravel. The Contact SL D-Fuse carbon handlebar and D-Fuse seatpost help smooth out rough tracks, improving control and minimizing fatigue over long distances.

KeyPerformanceFactors.Title: SMART INTEGRATION
KeyPerformanceFactors.Text: Internally routed cables offer clean, uncluttered cockpit aesthetics and improved aero performance. An easily accessed storage compartment inside the top tube lets you securely carry tools, snacks and other essentials. And additional integrated mounts on the top tube, fork and rear dropout make it easy to modify with additional packs or fenders.

Technologies.Name: Advanced Composite Technology
Technologies.Description: Giant’s High Performance Grade raw carbon material is used to produce this custom frame material in our own composite factory with a high stiffness-to-weight ratio. The front triangle of these framesets is assembled and molded as one continuous piece in a proprietary manufacturing process called Modified Monocoque Construction.

Technologies.Name: D-Fuse Technology
Technologies.Description: The D-Fuse seatpost and handlebar were designed to function as a system, absorbing road shocks and vibrations so you can ride farther, faster.

Technologies.Name: OverDrive
Technologies.Description: Giant’s original oversized fork steerer tube technology. Designed to provide precise front-end steering performance, the system’s oversized headset bearings (1 1/4” lower and 1 1/8” upper for road, 1 1/2” lower and 1 1/8” upper for mountain) and tapered steerer tube work in conjunction to provide optimal steering stiffness.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Technologies.Name: PowerCore
Technologies.Description: A massively oversized bottom-bracket/chainstay area features a fully integrated, 86-millimeter wide bottom-bracket design (92-millimeter wide for off-road bikes). Asymmetric chainstays provide additional stiffness on the driveside and stability on the non-driveside.

Technologies.Name: Flip Chip - Dropout
Technologies.Description: Allows riders to quickly adjust the geometry and rear-wheel tire clearance with eccentric, offset two-position hardware in the rear wheel dropouts.
